---
title: "Membership"
description: "How to become a member of ISA RC42 Social Psychology."
---

## Membership in RC42 Social Psychology

RC42 Social Psychology welcomes scholars and researchers from around the world who are interested in sociological social psychology.

### How to Join RC42

Membership in RC42 is managed through the International Sociological Association (ISA). To become a member of RC42, you need to:

1. **Be an ISA member** — Join the International Sociological Association through the [ISA website](https://www.isa-sociology.org/).
2. **Select RC42** — When registering or renewing your ISA membership, select RC42 Social Psychology as one of your Research Committees.

::: {.info-box}
ISA membership includes the option to join up to five Research Committees at no additional cost. Visit the [ISA membership page](https://www.isa-sociology.org/) for current fees and membership categories.
:::

### Who Can Join?

RC42 membership is open to:

- Faculty members and researchers in sociology and related disciplines
- Graduate students and early-career researchers
- Independent scholars and practitioners with an interest in sociological social psychology
- Researchers from any country or region

### Benefits of RC42 Membership

- Participate in RC42 sessions at ISA World Congresses
- Connect with an international network of scholars in sociological social psychology
- Receive the RC42 newsletter
- Be listed in the RC42 members directory (optional)
- Submit and vote on proposals for RC42 activities
- Stand for election to the RC42 board

### Contact

For questions about RC42 membership, contact: [isa-rc42@isa-sociology.org](mailto:isa-rc42@isa-sociology.org)
